risk_if_not_implemented: Without Session Lock, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Session Termination
compensating_control_1.description: Automated mechanisms exist to log out users, both locally on the network and for remote sessions, at the end of the session or after an organization-defined period of inactivity.
compensating_control_1.justification: Session Termination (IAC-25) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Session Lock (IAC-24) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Re-Authentication
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to force users and devices to re-authenticate according to organization-defined circumstances that necessitate re-authentication.
compensating_control_2.justification: Re-Authentication (IAC-14) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Session Lock (IAC-24) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
